methods.description: This method involves the study of nannofossil assemblages from marly interbeds in platform successions for biostratigraphy, using a standard smearing technique and semiquantitative analysis under a polarized light microscope.

steps.description: Study 13 new samples from marly interbeds in platform successions for nannofossil biostratigraphy.
steps.searchable_summary: collect nannofossil samples from specified locations

steps.description: Prepare samples using the standard smearing technique.
steps.searchable_summary: apply standard smearing technique to samples

steps.description: Perform semiquantitative analyses of nannofossil assemblages using a polarized light microscope under a magnification of 1250×, counting all specimens in 300 fields of view.

methods.description: This method measures the δ 13 C carb and δ 18 O carb isotopes in bulk rock samples using a mass spectrometer to understand carbon and oxygen isotope compositions.

steps.description: Measure δ 13 C carb and δ 18 O carb on 340 bulk rocks from specified sections using a ThermoFinnigan Gas Bench II coupled to a Delta V Plus mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure carbonate isotopes using mass spectrometer

steps.description: Report carbon and oxygen isotope compositions in δ notation relative to VPDB with reproducibility better than 0.1‰.
steps.searchable_summary: report isotope compositions in δ notation

methods.description: This method determines δ 13 C org in samples using a mass spectrometer after decarbonation, to analyze organic carbon isotope ratios.

steps.description: Determine δ 13 C org on 134 samples using a ThermoFisher Flash-EA coupled to a Delta V Plus mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure organic carbon isotopes using mass spectrometer

steps.description: Report isotope ratios in δ notation relative to VPDB with reproducibility better than ±0.2‰.
steps.searchable_summary: report organic carbon isotope ratios in δ notation

methods.description: This method models the global carbon cycle to compare the effects of volcanogenic and methane carbon release on the P-ToBE negative CIE.

steps.description: Model the measured −1.4‰ δ 13 C CIE of the Brasa section using a numerical model of the global carbon cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: model carbon cycle to simulate δ 13 C CIE

steps.description: Vary the δ 13 C of the additional carbon added to the ocean-atmosphere system and determine the quantity needed to generate the negative CIE observed.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate varying δ 13 C to determine carbon quantity

steps.description: Simulate a negative CIE amplitude of 2.0‰ to compare with other studied sections.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate negative CIE amplitude for comparison

steps.description: Model the P-To negative CIE using perturbation durations of 10,000, 50,000, and 100,000 years to evaluate their effect on carbon needed.
steps.searchable_summary: model CIE with varying perturbation durations
